sentence_reviews.text: Model servers expose trained models through batching, scheduling, caching, versioning, routing, and runtime-specific execution interfaces.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDIA supports service/system concerns and Transformer supports model architecture, but the cited sources do not directly document model serving features such as batching, versioning, routing, and inference runtime interfaces.
sentence_reviews.notes: Needs a model-serving/runtime source for direct support.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Model servers & inference runtimes is to connect Graph compilers, runtimes & operator fusion, Object storage, sharding & checkpoints to downstream capabilities including Batching, admission control & GPU scheduling, Speculative, parallel & assisted decoding, Tensor, pipeline, sequence & expert parallel inference.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support some underlying mechanisms, but this downstream capability list is atlas graph positioning rather than a directly sourced claim.
sentence_reviews.notes: Use a narrower claim or add sources for each downstream dependency if exact verification is required.
